rag_evidence.content:
SWC-107: Reentrancy

One of the major dangers of calling external contracts is that they can take over the control flow. In the reentrancy attack, a malicious contract calls back into the calling contract before the first invocation of the function is finished, so changes to state that should have prevented the re-call have not yet occurred. Remediation: finish all internal work (state changes) before calling external functions; use a reentrancy guard (mutex).

DeFi Security Incident: Phoenix
Date: 2023-03-01
Vulnerability type: access_control

Attack summary:
PhxProxy contract delegateCallSwap() function lack of access control and can be passed in any parameter
The lost money is mainly USDC in the d028 contract which the attacker converts into WETH in the 65ba contract through the buyLeverage function
and then swaps it into his own tokens by the delegateCallSwap function, making a profit from it

Post-mortem references:
https://twitter.com/HypernativeLabs/status/1633090456157401088

DeFi Security Incident: Paribus
Date: 2023-04-01
Vulnerability type: reentrancy

Attack summary:
a known reentrancy issue from the forked old version of CompoundV2

Post-mortem references:
https://twitter.com/Phalcon_xyz/status/1645742620897955842
https://twitter.com/BlockSecTeam/status/1645744655357575170
https://twitter.com/peckshield/status/1645742296904929280

DeFi Security Incident: Euler
Date: 2023-03-01
Vulnerability type: flash_loan

Attack summary:
1) Flash loan tokens from Balancer/Aave v2 => 30M DAI
2) Deploy two contracts: violator and liquidator
3) Deposit 2/3 of funds to Euler using deposit() => sent 20M DAI to Euler and received 19.5M eDAI from Euler
4) Borrow 10x of deposited amount using mint() => received 195.6M eDAI and 200M dDAI from Euler
5) Repay part of debt using the remaining 1/3 of funds using repay() => sent 10M DAI and burned 10M dDAI
6) Repeat 4th step => received 195.6M eDAI and 200M dDAI from Euler
7) Donate 10x of repaid funds using donateToReserves() => sent 100M eDAI to Euler
8)  Liquidate a violator’s account using liquidate() because eDAI < dDAI => received 310M eDAI and 259M dDAI of debt from the violator
9) Withdraw all token amount from Euler using withdraw() => withdrew 38.9M DAI from Euler
10) Repay flash loans

Post-mortem references:
https://twitter.com/FrankResearcher/status/1635241475989721089
https://twitter.com/nomorebear/status/1635230621856600064
https://twitter.com/peckshield/status/1635229594596036608
https://twitter.com/BlockSecTeam/status/1635262150624305153
https://twitter.com/SlowMist_Team/status/1635288963580825606

static_findings.description:
Reentrancy in Vault.withdraw(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_eo3lnqui.sol#9-14):
	External calls:
	- (ok,None) = msg.sender.call{value: amount}() (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_eo3lnqui.sol#11)
	State variables written after the call(s):
	- balances[msg.sender] -= amount (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_eo3lnqui.sol#13)
	Vault.balances (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_eo3lnqui.sol#3) can be used in cross function reentrancies:
	- Vault.balances (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_eo3lnqui.sol#3)
	- Vault.deposit() (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_eo3lnqui.sol#5-7)
	- Vault.withdraw(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_eo3lnqui.sol#9-14)
Consider introducing checks for `msg.sender` to ensure the recipient of the money is as intended.
Changing state after an external call can lead to re-entrancy attacks.Use the checks-effects-interactions pattern to avoid this issue.
Use descriptive reason strings or custom errors for revert paths.

Solc compiler version 0.8.20 switches the default target EVM version to Shanghai, which means that the generated bytecode will include PUSH0 opcodes. Be sure to select the appropriate EVM version in case you intend to deploy on a chain other than mainnet like L2 chains that may not support PUSH0, otherwise deployment of your contracts will fail.
There are state variable changes in this function but no event is emitted. Consider emitting an event to enable offchain indexers to track the changes.
Consider using a specific version of Solidity in your contracts instead of a wide version. For example, instead of `pragma solidity ^0.8.0;`, use `pragma solidity 0.8.0;`
If a function is marked public but is not used internally, consider marking it as `external`.

unused_evidence: 2 High/Medium static finding(s) not tied to a verdict: eth-send-unchecked-address, reentrancy-state-change
failure_modes: ExternalBug flagged — the ML model is known to over-predict this class (Run 12 class-definition mismatch); treat as lower confidence unless corroborated by an inter-contract call finding.
reflection_notes.summary: The audit exhibits significant internal inconsistency, particularly in areas where confidence is low or verdicts are disputed, such as Reentrancy (60% confidence) and IntegerUO. The existence of two high/medium static findings that were not explicitly tied to a final verdict suggests evidence was ignored or insufficiently processed. Furthermore, the failure mode explicitly notes that the ML model over-predicts the ExternalBug class, indicating potential overconfidence in that specific finding despite the uncertainty noted elsewhere. This pattern suggests a risk of overlooking critical, unverified evidence when finalizing the audit conclusions.
